Course_Title: Build Beautiful Cross-Platform Apps with Flutter
Course_GideLine_One: Participants must have access to a computer (Windows/Linux/Mac) with internet connectivity.
Course_GideLine_Two: No prior experience required — this course starts from the basics.
Course_GideLine_Three: Students must commit at least 6–8 hours per week for study and practice.
Course_GideLine_Four: Assignments will be given every week and must be submitted on time.
Course_GideLine_Five: Respect peer feedback and collaborate during group sessions.
Course_GideLine_Six: A final capstone project must be completed to earn the certificate.

Course_Title: Full-Stack Web Development – From HTML to Deployment
Course_GideLine_One: No prior experience required — this course starts from the basics.
Course_GideLine_Two: Students must commit at least 6–8 hours per week for study and practice.
Course_GideLine_Three: All sessions will be recorded and shared for later viewing.
Course_GideLine_Four: Weekly quizzes and coding tasks are part of the learning path.
Course_GideLine_Five: Final portfolio website must be submitted for certification.
Course_GideLine_Six: Collaboration and respectful behavior in the student community are mandatory.

Course_Title: Complete Digital Marketing Course – SEO, Social Media & Ads Mastery
Course_GideLine_One: No prior marketing experience needed – beginners are welcome.
Course_GideLine_Two: Students must create a Gmail account for accessing Google tools.
Course_GideLine_Three: Weekly practical tasks must be submitted to continue to the next module.
Course_GideLine_Four: A laptop or desktop is required for effective learning.
Course_GideLine_Five: Final project includes creating a digital marketing campaign for a real or mock business.
Course_GideLine_Six: Certification will be provided only upon full completion and project submission.

Course_Title: Professional Graphic Design Course – From Basics to Branding
Course_GideLine_One: No prior design experience is required — course starts from scratch.
Course_GideLine_Two: Students must have access to a PC or laptop with design software installed.
Course_GideLine_Three: Regular practice and project submission are key to progress.
Course_GideLine_Four: Course includes real-world client-based design tasks.
Course_GideLine_Five: Final portfolio submission is required for certification.
Course_GideLine_Six: Respect creative feedback and participate actively in peer reviews.

Course_Title: Become a UI/UX Designer – From User Research to Interactive Design
Course_GideLine_One: No prior design experience is required — course starts with foundational concepts.
Course_GideLine_Two: Participants must have access to a laptop or desktop with design software installed (Adobe XD, Figma, etc.).
Course_GideLine_Three: Active participation in design critique sessions and peer feedback is required.
Course_GideLine_Four: Weekly design exercises and challenges must be completed to develop practical skills.
Course_GideLine_Five: Final project includes creating a complete UI/UX design for a real or mock app/website.
Course_GideLine_Six: Students are encouraged to create a portfolio of their work during the course for future job opportunities.

Course_Title: Master Cyber Security – Protecting Systems and Data from Threats
Course_GideLine_One: Basic understanding of computer networks is recommended but not required.
Course_GideLine_Two: Participants must have access to a computer with a stable internet connection for hands-on labs.
Course_GideLine_Three: Ethical hacking tools will be used in a controlled and legal environment.
Course_GideLine_Four: Weekly quizzes, assignments, and virtual labs are part of the learning process.
Course_GideLine_Five: Final project includes simulating a real-world cyber attack and defending against it.
Course_GideLine_Six: Adherence to ethical guidelines is mandatory — hacking activities should be done only in safe environments.
